notes: Major additive release: retirement planning + flagship Dashboard. Strictly additions — existing pages (Budget, Journal, Projection, Net Worth, Scenarios, Instruments, EMI, Settings, Edit Inputs) and all engine math are untouched. (1) SCHEMA v14: new optional retirement fields (retEnabled default false, p1/p2CurrentAge, p1/p2RetireAge, lifeExpectancy, retExpensePct, postRetReturn, retStrategies map keyed by investment id). Migration is idempotent and defaults retirement OFF, so existing saved state upgrades cleanly with zero behavioral change until the user opts in. (2) ONBOARDING STEP 7 (Retirement): wizard grew from 8 to 9 steps (Rates -> 8, Review -> 9, cleanly renumbered across step-nodes, panels, showStep loop, saveStep cases, nav wiring, applyEditModeUI loop, and editData edit-cards). New step has an enable toggle (skippable — app behaves exactly as before if left off), separate current + retirement ages for both partners (partner fields auto-hide in solo mode), dynamic life-expectancy with a live timeline hint, post-retirement lifestyle % slider with live rupee preview, a separate post-retirement drawdown return, and full per-investment strategy cards (Hold / Withdraw / SWP / Partial / Annuity) with contextual sub-inputs. (3) SETTINGS: standalone retirement editor mirroring the onboarding step, with Apply that saves + recomputes. Also reachable via the Edit-inputs grid. (4) CORPUS ENGINE: computeRetirement(p, opts) returns null when retirement is off, else required corpus (growing-annuity present value: PMT x [1-((1+g)/(1+r))^n]/(r-g), g=inflation proxy from expenseGrowthRate, r=postRetReturn), projected corpus (net worth at the retirement month from the existing projection; optional what-if extraMonthly future-valued at blended investment rate), gap, pctFunded, PV-in-today's-money, a per-strategy income bridge (withdraw->pool, swp/annuity->passive monthly, partial->split, hold->estate), and a drawdown simulation to life expectancy yielding terminal value + exhaustion age. Pure read of state + projection; no engine mutation. (5) DASHBOARD (new flagship page; nav item placed first, section + render-map entry added; the prior no-op renderDashboard is replaced, legacy unused helper left untouched): hero net worth with composition bar + retirement corpus progress (or setup CTA when off), monthly pulse (income/outflow/surplus), net-worth trajectory chart with a dashed corpus-target line, what-if slider (Priority 3), liquidity health score in months-of-expenses (Priority 5), debt payoff tracker with per-EMI progress bars (Priority 4), asset & growth table, auto-generated qualitative insights (savings rate, EMI-to-income ratio, corpus gap with extra-monthly estimate, corpus exhaustion, concentration), and a Year-in-review comparison of net-worth change / income growth / savings rate (Priority 7). (6) NET WORTH CHART annotations (Priority 6): milestone markers for EMI payoff dates, retirement, and scenario starts overlaid on the existing Net Worth Over Time chart via a scatter dataset; base line/fill unchanged. Scope: display + new self-contained computation only. No change to projected balances, net worth values, or the shared savings chain (projectSavingsChain). KNOWN ITEM (still deliberately deferred, unchanged from prior release): the current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so logging a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings; that change touches projectSavingsChain and needs dedicated double-count verification before it is made.
